With the latest incident, 17 people have lost their lives in the district, while six others remain missing.In a separate operation, a joint team of the State Disaster Response Force (SDRF), National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) and the Indian Army carried out a rescue and recovery operation to retrieve a body from the Munawar River in the Nowshera area of Rajouri district.The fresh tragedy comes a day after torrential rain triggered flash floods and landslides across the Pir Panjal region, killing at least 14 people and leaving several others trapped under debris.Chief minister Omar Abdullah cut short his visit to New Delhi and flew to Jammu on Sunday to oversee relief and rescue operations. Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha also reviewed the situation and directed officials to provide immediate assistance to affected families.The heavy rain also disrupted traffic on the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway and led to the temporary suspension of the annual Amarnath Yatra and pilgrimages to the Vaishno Devi shrine and Shiv Khori after the India Meteorological Department forecast more adverse weather.Rescue operations involving the Army, police, SDRF, NDRF, civil administration and local volunteers are continuing in the worst-affected areas of Poonch and Rajouri as authorities search for the missing and assess the damage.
Article Summary: Officials say Poonch has been battered by torrential rain since Saturday evening. With the latest incident, 17 people have lost their lives in the district, while six others remain missing. Heavy rain also disrupted traffic on the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway.

Article Title: Govt, opposition face off on Day 1 of Monsoon Session: Key takeaways
Article Text:
Monsoon Session

Lok Sabha disrupted from the start

11 am

12.00 pm:

12.30 pm:

At 1 pm:

2.00 pm:

Adjourned at 3.00 pm:

Rajya Sabha also adjourned

11 am:

PM Modi appeals for productive session

Opposition attacks government

Priyanka slams police action on protesters

Tharoor: Parliament cannot become a notice board

SP chief Akhilesh backs protesting students

Protests outside Parliament

NEW DELHI: The opening day of Parliament's Monsoon Session descended into a government-versus-opposition showdown on Monday as repeated protests led to multiple adjournments before both the Houses were adjourned for the day.The opposition demanded discussions on the alleged NEET-UG paper leak and the alleged embezzlement of donations meant for the Ram Temple in Ayodhya, while the government pressed ahead with its legislative business amid the disruptions, according to news agency PTI.This development comes as large number of students and protesters attempted to march towards Parliament following a call by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), but were stopped by police, who used tear gas and carried out a lathi-charge after the crowd tried to breach barricades.: The Lok Sabha convened at 11 am, but proceedings were disrupted almost immediately as opposition members raised slogans and displayed placards, demanding discussions on the NEET-UG paper leak and the alleged Ram Temple donation scam.Speaker Om Birla objected to members raising slogans while obituary references for six former MPs were being read, calling it an inappropriate precedent. With protests continuing, the House was adjourned till 12 noon.When the House reassembled at 12 noon, the disruptions continued, forcing another adjournment till 12.30 pm.During the brief sitting, Union law minister Arjun Ram Meghwal introduced The Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026, which seeks to replace the ordinance increasing the sanctioned strength of Supreme Court judges from 34 to 38, including the Chief Justice of India.The House met again at 12.30 pm, but repeated appeals by the Chair to allow Zero Hour to function failed to calm protesting MPs. Presiding officer Dilip Saikia adjourned proceedings till 1 pm.Opposition protests continued. Saikia urged members to return to their seats, saying, "The House is ready for discussions on various issues. But you are not interested. You don't want to discuss people's issues. This is not the way.""You are disrupting the House repeatedly. You are showing banners and posters which is not acceptable," he added.As protests continued, the House was adjourned till 2 pm.When the Lok Sabha reconvened at 2 pm, presiding officer Jagdambika Pal said there was adequate quorum to conduct business, although opposition MPs alleged they were being prevented from entering the House. Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju maintained that the House could not function amid continuous disruptions.The House was adjourned again at 2.30 pm as opposition members continued protesting despite appeals to allow proceedings under Rule 377.After assembling six times during the day, the Lok Sabha was finally adjourned at around 3 pm and will reconvene at 11 am on Tuesday.The Rajya Sabha witnessed similar scenes, with opposition members demanding discussions on the same issues. Repeated disruptions prevented normal business, forcing the Upper House to adjourn for the day.It will also reconvene at 11 am on Tuesday.Earlier in the day, Rajya Sabha Chairman C.P. Radhakrishnan administered the oath to newly elected members, including senior Congress leader Pawan Khera, BJP MPs Dr Mukeshbhai J. Rathwa, Mahesh Kewat, NPP's James Pangsang Kongkal Sangma, and BJP's Prakash Chik Baraik.Minutes before Parliament convened, Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged MPs to ensure a productive Monsoon Session. "We hope that the monsoon and the Monsoon Session of Parliament are productive... I appeal to all MPs to wholeheartedly participate in proceedings of Monsoon session," he said."We are well aware, and the entire world is concerned about the persistent threat of war in various regions. The conflict in West Asia posed a major crisis for countries like India that rely on others for energy. Numerous obstacles and crises arose regarding petrol, diesel, LPG, fertilisers, and chemicals. Despite this, India maintained a growth rate of 7.7 per cent, emerging as the fastest-growing major economy in the world," he added.The prime minister also took a veiled swipe at leader of opposition in Loka Sabha, Rahul Gandhi while praising the young team behind Skyroot Aerospace's successful launch of India's first privately developed orbital rocket."I am told that the average age of the team working at the Skyroot start-up is just 28 years. It is these young minds who have made this possible. I am not talking about a 56-year-old young man; I am talking about a start-up whose team, with an average age of just 28, has planted India's flag in the realm of space," Modi said.Leader of the opposition in the Rajya Sabha Mallikarjun Kharge accused the government of suppressing democratic protests and demanded accountability over the alleged Ram Temple donation scam."Lathi-charge on students is an attack on democracy. This government is not capable of staying in power," Kharge said."We have also raised the issue of theft of donations (at Ayodhya Ram Temple). Modi sahib should take responsibility because he is the one who created the Trust," he added.Congress general secretary Priyanka Gandhi Vadra also criticised the Centre over the police action against students and protesters marching towards Parliament, accusing the government of refusing to discuss issues related to the education system while resorting to force."It's ridiculous... They are our own children... they are the future of the country. This Parliament belongs to them. It's no one's personal fiefdom," Priyanka Gandhi told reporters in the Parliament House complex."It is a genuine issue, Rahul ji has been raising this issue for quite some time. We have been saying there should be a discussion as there are problems in the education policy. You are not ready to discuss and fire tear gas at children and beat them up. For what? They are our children," she added.Congress MP Shashi Tharoor criticised the government for refusing to allow discussions on key issues. "Parliament is supposed to be the place where we can ventilate the voice of the people," he said."It cannot be a notice board for government to announce its bills and get them passed with a rubber stamp." Questioning the police action against protesters, Tharoor added, "Lathi-charge is not an act of non-violence, it is an act of violence. I don't understand the logic of it."Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav also targeted the government over the alleged NEET paper leak and the handling of student protests."They are keeping protesters in police stations and beating students outside. What kind of government is this that talks about 'Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas' but doesn't want to listen to children? Is it a government or is it your ego?" he asked.Questioning the credibility of the examination process, Yadav said, "If the NEET examination happened and there was cheating, who is responsible? What about the credibility?" He also said students protesting for justice should be treated with dignity.The political confrontation extended beyond Parliament as protesters led by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) attempted to march from Jantar Mantar towards Parliament, demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET paper leak.Police stopped the march after protesters attempted to breach barricades, using mild force to disperse the crowd. Security around Parliament was tightened, with several entry gates closed and additional personnel deployed.Earlier in the day, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke ended his indefinite hunger strike at the request of activist Sonam Wangchuk , who remains admitted to Safdarjung Hospital after his own hunger strike.The session, which will continue till August 13, is expected to witness intense exchanges as the opposition presses the government on a host of issues ahead of key state elections.
Article Summary: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha adjourned for the day. Opposition demands discussions on alleged NEET-UG paper leak and alleged embezzlement of donations meant for the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. Prime Minister Narendra Modi urges MPs to ensure a productive Monsoon Session.

Article Title: 'Where did your 56-inch chest go?' Kharge hits back at PM Modi's '56-year-old youth' jab
Article Text:
Rajya Sabha LoP Mallikarjun Kharge hits back at PM Modi

Kharge hits back at PM Modi

Rajya Sabha adjourned

NEW DELHI: Congress president and leader of opposition in Rajya Sabha, Mallikarjun Kharge , has launched a scathing attack on PM Modi on Monday over his vieled criticism of Rahul Gandhi.He condemned the police baton charge on students at Jantar Mantar and demanded accountability from Prime Minister Narendra Modi.Kharge responded to PM Modi's veiled swipe at Rahul Gandhi, in which the Prime Minister praised young innovators while saying he was "not talking about any 56-year-old 'youngster.'""PM Modi's only job is to taunt others and make snide remarks. First, prove your own credibility to the people. Where did that '56-inch chest' of yours go?" Kharge said.He accused the PM of deflecting from governance failures. "You criticise others today, but I ask: what have you actually done for the country? You told your followers to form a trust so they could engage in theft, and now they are stealing rampantly. You enacted the laws and nominated the people for the Ram Mandir Trust, and your own people are involved in it."He also condemned the statements made by the PM, “Every Prime Minister talks about resolving problems, not making remarks that invite ridicule later. I condemn this statement, and PM Modi should refrain from making such comments in the future”Kharge also raised the issue of alleged examination irregularities, saying lakhs of students were in distress."Who is responsible for destroying their lives? The Modi government and PM Modi himself. He does these things just to hide his own weaknesses.""As long as they remain, the country won't see prosperity. Prosperity will only come once they are gone," he said.The Rajya Sabha was adjourned for the day as opposition called out the government for suppressing the Cockroach Janta Party protest at Jantar Mantar, where thousands had gathered demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan."I am talking about the future of lakhs of students. For that, thousands of children have come to Jantar Mantar. There has been a baton charge. The government is trying to hurt them. It is trying to suppress them," Kharge said amid sloganeering.Thousands gathered at Jantar Mantar on Monday ahead of the CJP's planned march to Parliament to demand Pradhan's resignation. Police resorted to a baton charge to disperse the crowd and denied permission for the march, imposing prohibitory orders across much of New Delhi.
Article Summary: Congress president and leader of opposition in Rajya Sabha, Mallikarjun Kharge, has launched a scathing attack on PM Modi. He condemned the police baton charge on students at Jantar Mantar and demanded accountability from PM Narendra Modi. Kharge responded to PM Modi's veiled swipe at Rahul Gandhi.

Article Title: 'Got no commitments': CJP leaders handover 3 demands to JP Nadda; minister says end 'sit-in'
Article Text:
'Assist administration in restoring normalcy'

Amit Shah meets Pradhan

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you support the ongoing protests by the Cockroach Janata Party? Yes, I support them No, I do not support them 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: A delegation of Cockroach Janata Party met Union minister JP Nadda on Monday and submitted its petition demanding accountability from the government over the NEET-UG paper leak case.According to CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka, the meeting with Nadda was under way, with the Union minister seeking time to consult the government leadership on the party's demands.The demands include the immediate release of activist Sonam Wangchuk, the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, and Rs 1 crore compensation for the families of NEET aspirants who died by suicide."Me and Saurav Das are still at Nadda ji's residence. Nadda ji has requested some time to align with their leadership with regards to our following demands: Immediate release of Sonam Wangchuk, Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation, Rs 1 crore compensation for all NEET aspirants who committed suicide," Ranka said in a post on X.CJP spokesperson Sourav said while Nadda assured them that he will discuss the demands at the appropriate level, but not commitments have been given so far.He added that the protest will continue "until the demand is met.""Ashutosh Ranka and I have been at JP Nadda’s residence since 12 noon. The demands have been conveyed, including immediate resignation/sacking of Dharmendra Pradhan," Das said."The minister assured us he will discuss this at the appropriate level.However, no commitments have been made so far. The peaceful protesters will not rest until the demand is met!" he added.According to Das, Nadda also assured the delegation that no further crackdown will happen at CJP's protest site.After the meeting, Nadda said that the meeting took place in a cordial atmosphere and requested all the protestors to end their protest and "assist the administration in restoring normalcy.""This morning, for the first time, a proposal came from the protesters to hold talks with the government, and our discussions have been ongoing since 11:50 AM," Nadda said in a social media post on X."The meeting took place in a cordial atmosphere. An initial verbal discussion was held in detail with their delegation, and they submitted a written petition to me around 4 PM. I have requested all the protesters to end their sit-in and assist the administration in restoring normalcy," he added."The minister has also assured us that no further crackdown will happen at Jantar Mantar and around. However, no reports yet of police stopping," he added.Meanwhile, Union home minister Amit Shah met Pradhan at his office in the Parliament House complex. Officials did not disclose what transpired during the meeting.This comes as thousands tried to march to Parliament as part of the Cockroach Janta Party's protest call to demand the former's resignation over alleged examination irregularities.Outside Parliament, police used tear gas and mild force after demonstrators allegedly attempted to breach barricades and march towards the Parliament complex. Several protesters were detained.Security was tightened across central Delhi, with Rapid Action Force personnel deployed at key locations to prevent any breach. As a precautionary measure, several gates at Metro stations in the area were temporarily closed, affecting commuters.At least seven CJP protesters, including Abhijeet Dipke, were detained near the Reserve Bank of India, close to Parliament, during the party's 'Sansad Chalo' march, PTI reported.
Article Summary: A delegation of Cockroach Janata Party met Union minister JP Nadda on Monday. They submitted its petition demanding accountability from the government over the NEET-UG paper leak case. The demands include the immediate release of activist Sonam Wangchuk, the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, and Rs 1 crore compensation for the families of NEET aspirants who died by suicide.

Article Title: 'Lacklustre, deliberately understated event': PDP takes swipe at NC's Delhi protest for J&K statehood
Article Text:
PDP leader Iltija Mufti (File photo/PTI)

NEW DELHI: As the ruling National Conference (NC) staged a protest in Delhi demanding the restoration of statehood to Jammu and Kashmir, Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) leader Iltija Mufti on Monday dismissed the demonstration as a "lacklustre and deliberately understated one-day event. "She argued that had the protest been held at Jantar Mantar—the capital's designated venue for demonstrations—it would have echoed with slogans demanding the restoration of the Union Territory's special status."Instead of a lacklustre, deliberately understated and soft-pedalled one-day event at a random Delhi club without even a whimper about Article 370, imagine a protest in Jantar Mantar reverberating with slogans for reinstituting Jammu and Kashmir's Special Status," PTI quoted Iltija Mufti, daughter of PDP president and former chief minister Mehbooba Mufti, as saying.Calling it "unfortunate", Iltija Mufti claimed that the elected government in Jammu and Kashmir was attempting to "normalise" what she described as the "illegal and disempowering" abrogation of Article 370.The PDP, despite being invited by the NC, stayed away from the protest after laying down conditions for its participation. The party had insisted that the agitation's agenda include demands for the restoration of Article 370, the release of political prisoners, and the lifting of the ban on Jamaat-e-Islami.In a letter to NC president Farooq Abdullah on Saturday, Mehbooba Mufti explained that the party had decided against joining a protest focused solely on the restoration of statehood."After careful consideration and deliberations with my senior colleagues, I have come to the conclusion that it would not be fit for us to participate in a protest whose raison d'etre is solely and exclusively the demand for statehood," she wrote.According to the letter, the PDP would participate in a protest at Jantar Mantar only if the restoration of Article 370, the release of political prisoners, and the lifting of the ban on Jamaat-e-Islami formed the core of its agenda.On August 5, 2019, the Narendra Modi government revoked Article 370, which had granted special status to Jammu and Kashmir. In October that year, the erstwhile state was bifurcated into the Union territories of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh.
Article Summary: PDP leader Iltija Mufti dismisses Delhi protest as a "lacklustre and deliberately understated one-day event" She claims the elected government in Jammu and Kashmir was attempting to "normalise" what she described as the "illegal and disempowering" abrogation of Article 370.

Article Title: Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar found dead at Agartala police headquarters
Article Text:
Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar found dead at police headquarters in Agartala

Who was Anurag Dhankar?



NEW DELHI: Tripura Director General of Police (DGP) Anurag Dhankar was found dead at his office at the state police headquarters in Agartala on Monday, officials said.Following the incident, chief minister Manik Saha, chief secretary J K Sinha and other senior government officials visited the hospital, news agency ANI reported. The cause of death was not immediately known.The circumstances surrounding his death are under investigation, and further details are awaited.Anurag Dhankar, a 1994-batch IPS officer of the Tripura cadre, took over as the state's Director General of Police (DGP) in May 2025 after serving as DGP (Intelligence).A highly decorated officer with over three decades of service, Dhankar was awarded the Police Medal for Meritorious Service and the President's Police Medal for Distinguished Service.He began his career in Tripura in 1995 and served in several key positions, including SDPO of Longtharai Valley, Superintendent of Police of the undivided West and South Tripura districts, Superintendent of Police (Special Branch) and AIGP (Headquarters).Dhankar also served with the United Nations Mission in Kosovo during 2003-04, gaining international policing experience.He spent several years on central deputation. Between 2005 and 2013, he served as SP and DIG in the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI).After returning to Tripura, he was appointed IGP (Law and Order) from 2013 to 2016.From 2016 to 2023, he was again on central deputation, serving as IG (Research and Correctional Administration) in the Bureau of Police Research and Development (BPR&D), IG (Personnel) in the Central Industrial Security Force (CISF), and later as Joint Director and Additional Director in the CBI.Dhankar also headed the Special Investigation Team (SIT) constituted by the ministry of home affairs to probe the 1984 anti-Sikh riots.
Article Summary: Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar found dead at police headquarters in Agartala. Chief minister Manik Saha, chief secretary J K Sinha and other senior government officials visited the hospital. The circumstances surrounding his death are under investigation, and further details are awaited.

Article Title: NTA names 5 NEET candidates, says OMR claims built on digital forgeries
Article Text:
NTA dismisses discrepancy allegations regarding OMR sheets (Photo: PTI)

Poll Vote & Share your view What is your opinion on the use of AI in generating altered examination documents? It should be strictly prohibited It has benefits if regulated 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: The National Testing Agency on Monday publicly rebutted the claims of five NEET-UG candidates alleging discrepancies in their OMR answer sheets, saying the images circulated by or on their behalf had been digitally altered, regenerated using AI or manipulated by adding responses that were not present on the original sheets.The detailed case-wise clarification came a day after TOI reported that NTA had, for the first time, detected AI-generated OMR sheets among complaints challenging NEET-UG 2026 results. The agency had said no error in the declared results had been established so far and warned that candidates using fabricated records could face cancellation of candidature, debarment and criminal proceedings.The results were declared on July 16 after the June 21 re-examination, ordered following the cancellation of the original May test over a question-paper leak. Several candidates and parents had alleged that uploaded OMR sheets were interchanged or showed fewer attempted answers than claimed.In the case of Avaneesh Srivastava, NTA said the genuine OMR bearing his name, parents’ names, signature, thumb impression and invigilators’ signatures was on record and had also been emailed to him during the response-key challenge window. It said the circulated image carried the identity of “Ajeet Singh”, a person with the stated parentage who did not exist in the NEET-UG 2026 database.According to the agency, the printed portion had been regenerated through AI- or OCR-based tools and the identity fields overwritten. Srivastava’s score of 337 was verified, it said.NTA similarly rejected Pratibha Trivedi’s claim, saying the circulated image was a significantly altered version of her genuine OMR and her score of 38 stood. In Abhay Yadav’s case, the agency said his public claim that he had left only five questions unanswered was contradicted by the official OMR, which showed 44 unattempted questions. His score of 164 was verified.For Lakshya Singh, NTA said additional answer bubbles had been digitally shaded on the circulated image. The genuine sheet showed 54 attempts — 34 correct and 20 incorrect — with 126 questions unattempted, resulting in 116 marks. In Arya Singh’s case, it said the invigilator’s signing time had been altered from 3.45pm to 2.45pm, along with other digital changes. Her score of 167 stood.In a public notice, NTA said genuine examination documents carry barcodes, booklet and answer-sheet numbers and, in scorecards, QR codes linked to authoritative server records. “A fabricated image may deceive the eye, but it does not, and cannot, alter the record on those servers,” it said.The agency said forged OMRs or scorecards could attract action under the Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and the Information Technology Act. It, however, said candidates could continue to raise genuine grievances through the prescribed channel.
Article Summary: National Testing Agency publicly rebutted claims of five NEET-UG candidates alleging discrepancies in their OMR answer sheets. Agency said images circulated by or on their behalf had been digitally altered, regenerated using AI or manipulated by adding responses that were not present on the original sheets.

Article Title: 'Abhishek Banerjee refused to step aside': Madan Mitra quits Mamata's camp, joins Ritabrata Banerjee's TMC faction
Article Text:
Madan Mitra, Ritabrata Banerjee, Mamata Banerjee

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you believe the TMC will recover from the recent defections? Yes, they will regain strength No, it's too late for recovery 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress (TMC) MLA Madan Mitra on Wednesday resigned from the Mamata Banerjee-led faction of the party and joined the rebel TMC faction headed by Ritabrata Banerjee, dealing another setback to the former chief minister's camp.His exit comes amid a series of defections from the Mamata Banerjee-led faction, with several senior leaders and MLAs joining the rival group led by Ritabrata Banerjee in recent weeks following the political split in the party.Speaking to reporters after joining the rebel camp, Mitra accused TMC national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee of refusing to step aside despite the party's crisis."I had suggested to Abhishek Banerjee that he step aside for six months or a year. I told him, let us build the party, and then you can come back and take your seat, but he refused. He said, 'I won't leave the party.' The party is sinking; the boat has gone under. People are dying. Yet, the party decided, or rather it was forced to accept, that everyone else could die, but Abhishek had to be saved. It is deeply saddening."Mitra alleged that the party had become centred around one individual instead of functioning collectively."The party belongs to everyone, yet it seems to have been reduced to serving only Abhishek. I earnestly request Mamata ji to come. Let us view this as a marathon. We will surely cross paths along the way. Let us see which horse surges ahead."He said he had resigned from all organisational positions in the Trinamool Congress and no longer considered himself part of the party."I have resigned from all the positions. Though I remain an MLA. I have left everything associated with Trinamool. That means I am no longer a Trinamool MLA in the functional sense.""I have only changed my room, not my house. I am very much in the TMC," Mitra told reporters after meeting the rebel leader.The development comes a day after the Enforcement Directorate (ED) summoned Mitra's wife and his two sons for questioning in connection with its money laundering investigation into the alleged municipal recruitment scam in West Bengal.According to the ED, the summons were issued after investigators found financial transactions involving the family members during the course of the probe. The agency has asked them to appear next week to record their statements.The case relates to alleged irregularities in appointments to various municipal bodies in West Bengal. The ED is investigating the matter under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) based on predicate offences registered by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI).The agency had earlier conducted searches at multiple premises linked to Mitra in June this year, while the CBI had also searched his residence in October 2025 as part of its investigation into the same case.
Article Summary: Madan Mitra has joined the rebel TMC faction headed by Ritabrata Banerjee. His exit comes amid a series of defections from the former chief minister's camp. The Enforcement Directorate has summoned Mitra's wife and his two sons for questioning in connection with its money laundering investigation.

Article Title: Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar who had led Nirav, anti-Sikh riots probes found dead in office toilet
Article Text:
Tripura oppn seeks judicial probe into DGP’s death

AGARTALA: Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar , a 1994-batch IPS officer who led the CBI probe into the Rs 13,000-crore Punjab National Bank fraud case involving diamantaire Nirav Modi and later headed an SIT that reinvestigated the 1984 anti-Sikh riots, was found dead in his office toilet at Agartala police HQ Monday.Govt has ordered a magisterial probe amid demands for a judicial inquiry. Police said circumstances pointed to suicide but declined to confirm the cause of death or say whether any death note had been found.Police sources said Dhankar, 60, remained inside the toilet unusually long, prompting his orderly to raise an alarm. The door, locked from inside, was broken open. He was taken to Agartala govt medical college and hospital around noon.Dr Pradip Bhowmik said Dhankar was brought to the hospital with no signs of life. “He was dead. There was no scope for any treatment,” he said. Hospital sources said doctors nevertheless attempted resuscitation for nearly 48 minutes before declaring him dead.Tripura CM Manik Saha rushed to police headquarters after being informed and later chaired an emergency cabinet meeting. “Investigation has already started and govt will follow whatsoever is required to uncover the truth,” Saha said.The autopsy, forensic tests and inquiry would examine all possible angles, including personal, professional and administrative factors, before drawing conclusions. Tripura govt declared an one-day state mourning Tuesday and appointed additional DGP Goduguluri Srinivasa Rao, a 1999-batch IPS officer, as the acting police chief.Dhankar took charge as Tripura DGP in May 2025 after serving as DGP (Intelligence). Over three decades in service, he served with CBI and BSF between 2005 and 2013 and UN Mission in Kosovo in 2003-04. He is survived by his wife, and a son in Canada.Dhankar held a postgraduate degree in food technology from CFTRI, Mysuru, a master’s degree in police management from Osmania University and a PG diploma in cybercrime from NALSAR University of Law in Hyderabad.Tripura BJP president Abhishek Debroy said investigators would examine all aspects of the case, including reports of an alleged suicide note.Opposition parties demanded an independent judicial inquiry. Leader of opposition Jitendra Chaudhury urged govt to preserve the body until Dhankar’s son arrived from Canada. “He rose from SDPO to DGP, showing his skill, integrity and dedication toward his responsibilities. He faced many challenges in his career...I don’t believe an honest and experienced officer would end his life without a major reason,” Chaudhury said.Congress MLA Sudip Roy Barman sought an HCsupervised investigation, and claimed a police officer had told him Dhankar was allegedly under mental stress before his death.
Article Summary: Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar was found dead in his office toilet at Agartala police HQ Monday. Police said circumstances pointed to suicide but declined to confirm the cause of death. Tripura govt declared an one-day state mourning Tuesday and appointed additional DGP Goduguluri Srinivasa Rao as the acting police chief.

Article Title: Looksmaxxing creator Hullo reportedly returns to TikTok after 15 CSAM charges
Article Text:
Image/X

Hullo reportedly starts posting on TikTok again after legal trouble

Mason Hull’s legal case continues as charges remain unresolved

Looksmaxxing creator Hullo, whose real name is Mason Hull, has reportedly returned to TikTok and started posting videos again around two months after he was charged with 15 counts of possession of child sexual abuse material (CSAM). His return has created a lot of discussion online, with many people reacting to his comeback. However, it is important to understand that he has only been charged and has not been found guilty. The legal case against him is still going on.Hullo became popular on the internet by making looksmaxxing videos. This type of content usually talks about improving appearance through things like exercise, grooming, hairstyle, and fashion. Over time, he gained many followers who watched his videos about self-improvement.According to reports, Hullo’s TikTok account is active again, and he has started sharing content after being away from the platform. His reported return has surprised many people because it happened only a short time after he faced serious legal charges.Many social media users have shared their opinions about his return. Some people questioned why someone facing criminal charges could continue posting online.Others said that a person who has only been charged is not automatically considered guilty and that the court process must decide the outcome.Social media platforms often have their own rules about accounts involved in serious situations. However, unless a platform removes an account or a court places restrictions, a person may still be able to use social media.So far, there has been no public statement from TikTok explaining Hullo’s reported return or whether the platform has taken any action regarding his account.Mason Hull was charged with 15 counts of possession of child sexual abuse material after an investigation by authorities. The charges brought major attention because Hull was already known online as a creator.This is an ongoing process within the legal system, and no verdict has been made yet. The mere fact that one is accused of an offense doesn’t imply guilt. There will be a hearing where all the evidence will be analyzed.Hullo’s comeback on TikTok has caused more discussions about internet creators and social media policies regarding individuals who are under investigation for crimes.Some people think that creators who have been charged with serious offenses shouldn’t build their audience online, whereas others think that verdicts shouldn’t be made by social media platforms before the trial takes place.For now, Hullo’s reported TikTok return remains a topic of discussion, while the legal case against him is still ongoing. The final outcome will depend on what happens during the court proceedings.
Article Summary: Mason Hull was charged with 15 counts of possession of child sexual abuse material. His TikTok account is active again, and he has started sharing content. Hullo’s comeback on TikTok has caused more discussions about internet creators and social media policies regarding individuals who are under investigation for crimes.

Article Title: Watch: Vietnam sleeper bus with passengers inside bursts into flames; 7 killed
Article Text: At least seven passengers were killed and five others injured after a passenger bus caught fire in southern Vietnam early Tuesday.According to Vietnam's ministry of public security, the 24-seat bus was travelling from Lam Dong province to Ho Chi Minh City when it suddenly caught fire. The flames spread rapidly, trapping several passengers inside the vehicle, cites Reuters.Several videos circulating on social media showed massive orange flames engulfing the sleeper bus. Firefighting tenders battled the blaze and emergency responders worked to bring the fire under control. The bus was completely gutted in the incident.Police have launched an investigation to determine the cause of the fire. Authorities are examining the circumstances that led to the blaze while rescue and relief efforts continue.
Article Summary: The 24-seat bus was travelling from Lam Dong province to Ho Chi Minh City when it suddenly caught fire. The flames spread rapidly, trapping several passengers inside the vehicle. Police have launched an investigation to determine the cause of the fire.

Article Title: Four Indians killed in Russian strike on vessel
Article Text:
Houthis declare naval blockade on Saudi...

NEW DELHI: Four Indian nationals were reported to have died on Sunday in an attack on a commercial vessel - MV Golden Leo - while it was departing the port of Odesa in Ukraine. At the time of the incident, there were 17 crew members on board, including 5 Indian nationals, according to the Indian govt.India condemned the attack, reiterating that targeting commercial shipping and endangering the lives of civilian crew members, or otherwise impeding freedom of navigation and commerce, is deplorable and must be avoided.Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said on Monday they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, a move that opens a new front against the US in its war on Iran and widens the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf. The Houthis did not say how they planned to impose the embargo.Iran had pressed the Houthis to close the Bab el-Mandeb gateway to the Red Sea if the US continued to attack Iranian power infrastructure.In their statement, the Houthis' armed forces said they were declaring "a maritime embargo against the criminal Saudi enemy, based on the equation of 'an eye for an eye', effective immediately..." They said the decision was in response to what they called "an unjust and oppressive siege" imposed on Yemen by the Saudis.There was no immediate response from Saudi Arabia.The full closure of the Bab el-Mandeb strait would reduce global oil supply by 7% as it would leave most of Saudi oil exports unable to leave the region. The disruption would add to the massive cut to global oil flows from the war in the Gulf, which has already reduced shipments by 10% of global supply."The question now is whether they will begin targeting vessels heading to Saudi ports. At this stage, there's no clear answer," said Mohammed Albasha, a Yemeni analyst and founder of Basha Report, a Washington-based risk advisory firm. "Even if no ships are attacked, the announcement alone is likely to disrupt shipping and create uncertainty for Saudi ports. Whether the Houthis move from threats to direct action remains the critical issue," he said.Saudi Arabia and the Houthis traded fire last week for the first time in years, threatening a 2022 truce that has held despite expiring. The exchange of fire was set off when an Iranian plane arriving from Tehran attempted to land in Sanaa, challenging Saudi Arabia's hegemony over Yemeni skies.The Houthis have previously tried to cut off shipping through the Red Sea. They previously launched drone and missile attacks on ships transiting the strategic waterway beginning in Nov 2023 during the Gaza war, forcing vessels to take lengthy detours around Africa instead of travelling directly from Europe. Yemen shares a border and coastline with Saudi Arabia, whose Red Sea port of Yanbu has been crucial for exporting oil, bypassing Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz during. agencies
Article Summary: Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said on Monday they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia. The move opens a new front against the US in its war on Iran and widens the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf. The Houthis did not say how they planned to impose the embargo.

Article Title: Missing for 2 days, national shooter Damayanti Sen found safe in Kolkata
Article Text:
Missing national level shooter Damayanti Sen returns home (FIle Photo)

HOWRAH: Fifteen-year-old national-level shooter Damayanti Sen, whose disappearance from her Howrah home on Friday morning sparked widespread concern on social media, was found on Saturday morning near Ramkrishnapur Ghat, police and family members said.Damayanti had left home on Friday morning, reportedly to buy milk, but did not return. Her absence triggered panic among relatives and prompted appeals for help online. Police later traced her movements through CCTV footage, which showed her at Howrah Station and later at the Rathyatra in Mahesh, Serampore.On Saturday morning, two morning walkers recognised her as she wandered near the Ramkrishnapur Ferry Jetty and alerted her family. Police said she was found barely a kilometre from her home, sitting in a corner of the riverside ghat under Howrah police jurisdiction. According to police, she appeared to be deeply absorbed in meditation when located, adding to the mystery surrounding the case.Family members said Damayanti later indicated that she had travelled to several places after leaving home because she had been under intense pressure while trying to balance academics and shooting.A Class X student of Diocesan School in Kolkata, she is known to be academically bright and a talented shooter.Her father, Dhruvajyoti Sen, currently head teacher of Andul Mahiyari Ranibala Kundu Choudhury Girls’ High School, told those close to the family that his daughter had recently been under mental strain. He also said she had shown signs of a “spiritual shift” in recent days. According to him, she had mentioned the name Baphomet once, but after being scolded, never raised it again. Family members said she had at times spoken of being “called” by the entity.Baphomet is a goat-headed, anthropomorphic figure rooted in Western occultism and esotericism. Historically, the name dates back to the 14th-century Knights Templar trials. However, its iconic modern depiction—a winged, hermaphroditic goat with arms bearing the alchemical terms Solve and Coagula—was created by the French occultist Éliphas Lévi in 1856. It is often described in occult literature as a symbol associated with secret worship and is also known as the official symbol of the Church of Satan.Senior Trinamool Congress leader and local MLA Arup Roy, who knows the family, said, “There is no doubt that Damayanti is a very good girl, very good in studies and highly talented in shooting. But she is somewhat spiritually inclined. Her father had told me several times that she occasionally became overly absorbed or excitable about certain religious matters. I was very worried when she went missing. I feel relieved she is back safe. ”Dhruvajyoti Sen has said he plans to arrange psychological counselling for his daughter with an expert counsellor to help reduce her stress.Police said further procedure would be followed before Damayanti is allowed to return home. “Damayanti will be produced in court in the afternoon. After that, if she wishes to return home, she will be handed over to her parents. Otherwise, she will be sent to a home as per procedure,” police stated.
Article Summary: Damayanti had left home on Friday morning, reportedly to buy milk, but did not return. Her absence triggered panic among relatives and prompted appeals for help online. Police traced her movements through CCTV footage, which showed her at Howrah Station and later at the Rathyatra in Mahesh, Serampore.

Article Title: US identifies two soldiers killed in Jordan, launches attack on Iran for 10th night
Article Text:
US identifies two soldiers killed in Jordan

US military deaths

rise amid widening conflict

US retaliates

The United States, on Monday, identified the two Army soldiers killed while defending against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks in Jordan, marking the first American troop deaths caused by direct Iranian fire since the opening days of the conflict.The US Department of Defense said First Lieutenant Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Hawaii, and Private Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Texas, were killed in the attack carried out on Friday.The military said Gonzales died on Friday, while Feehan succumbed to his injuries a day later. US Central Command (CENTCOM) also confirmed that a third service member remains missing following the attack.The Jordan attack highlights the growing risks faced by American forces stationed across the Middle East, even without large-scale ground operations inside Iran.On Saturday, another US service member was killed in northern Iraq during a controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance recovered from a downed Iranian drone. A second soldier was injured and is receiving treatment for minor wounds.The latest fatalities take the number of US service members killed since the war began to 17, according to the US military.President Donald Trump continues to defend the military campaign, saying it is necessary to prevent Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons.The United States military said late Monday it was carrying out its 10th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, intensifying operations aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz after another American service member was killed and Tehran launched attacks on US allies Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain.The US Central Command (CENTCOM) said on social media that the latest strikes "are designed to further degrade Iranian military capabilities used to attack commercial shipping" in the strategic waterway.The latest escalation came as President Donald Trump warned of a stronger response to attacks on American troops.
Article Summary: First Lieutenant Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Hawaii, and Private Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Texas, were killed in the attack carried out on Friday. On Saturday, another US service member was killed in northern Iraq during a controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance recovered from a downed Iranian drone.

Article Title: 'There won't be any more elections': Nicaragua President Ortega ends hope for democratic vote
Article Text:
'There won't be any more elections': Nicaragua President Ortega (AFP image)

"There won't be any more elections,"

Power consolidated since return to office

Opposition says move confirms

one-party rule

Nicaraguan President Daniel Ortega has declared that the country will not hold elections in the future, effectively ending hopes of a democratic vote and reinforcing his grip on power after nearly two decades in office.Speaking during celebrations marking the anniversary of the Sandinista Revolution on Sunday, Ortega said,while accusing the United States of backing political parties opposed to his government. "Never, never, never," he added when referring to future elections.The announcement effectively cancels Nicaragua's next presidential election, which had been expected in November 2027 after the government-controlled Congress extended the presidential term by one year in 2025.Ortega first came to power after the 1979 Sandinista Revolution and returned as president in 2007. Since then, he has rewritten the Constitution, expanded executive powers and tightened control over key state institutions, including the judiciary and electoral authorities.His government has faced widespread international criticism since a violent crackdown on anti-government protests in 2018 that left hundreds dead.In the 2021 presidential election, Ortega secured another term after the arrest of leading opposition candidates, prompting several countries, including the United States, to reject the legitimacy of the vote.Opposition leaders and human rights organisations described Ortega's latest announcement as a formal abandonment of democratic processes.The U.N. Human Rights Council also accused Ortega and Murillo's government of transforming the country "into an authoritarian state" and systematically violating human rights. In 2018, Ortega responded to widespread anti-government protests with a crackdown in which more than 300 people were killed.
Article Summary: Nicaraguan President Daniel Ortega has declared that the country will not hold elections in the future. The announcement effectively cancels Nicaragua's next presidential election, which had been expected in November 2027. The government- controlled Congress extended the presidential term by one year in 2025. The U.N. Human Rights Council has accused the government of transforming the country into an authoritarian state.

Article Title: Protests rock Dhaka as minority leaders condemn arrest of Hindu man building 81-ft Lord Ram statue
Article Text:
Pattern of persecution

Poll Vote & Share your view What is your opinion on the potential construction of the Lord Ram statue? It should be built as a symbol of unity. It may provoke further religious tensions. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

Minority leaders in Bangladesh on Saturday staged a large protest outside the National press club in Dhaka, condemning the arrest of a young Hindu man, Haridas Chandra Tarani Das, and alleging that the money laundering charges against him were fabricated to stop the construction of an 81-foot Lord Ram statue in Gaibandha district.The demonstration, organised by the Bangladesh Hindu-Buddhist-Christian unity council, brought together leaders from the Hindu, Buddhist and Christian communities, who accused the authorities of targeting religious minorities and demanded Das' immediate release.Speaking to ANI, Manindra Kumar Nath, general secretary of the Bangladesh Hindu-Buddhist-Christian Unity Council, strongly criticised the arrest. "Today, Bangladesh Hindu Buddha Krishnalok Tabarishad organised a demonstration against the arrest of Haridas Chandra Tarani Das. Haridas Chandra Tarani Das was arrested by the government some two or three days ago over the claim that he built a Ram Chandra Devata, Ram Chandra statue in Palashbari. In that case, we cannot accept this sort of arrest of Taroni Das."Nath said the arrest reflected a wider pattern of violence and legal action against minority communities in Bangladesh."You know, from the very beginning of the last two years, different types of arrests and atrocities have happened in Bangladesh. Not only that, you know, 3,000 incidents happened all over Bangladesh in the last year. Out of these, 66 were murdered, and there were so many temples attacked by the fundamentalists. It is undesirable. We cannot accept it."According to protest organisers, Das was arrested by law enforcement authorities before being remanded to judicial custody by a local court. Minority leaders alleged the money laundering case was intended to halt the construction of the proposed Lord Ram statue in Palashbari, Gaibandha.The arrest comes weeks after protests by members of the Hindu community over the reported desecration of an image of Lord Ram during an Islamist demonstration in the same district.Addressing the gathering, senior unity council leader Subrata Chowdhury questioned the circumstances surrounding the incident and urged Bangladesh's interim administration to identify those responsible. "A beautiful environment has been created, an environment of harmony has been established. In such a situation, who are they who have caused this kind of incident to embarrass this government? We want to give an ultimatum to the government to take proper action against this. "He also demanded the immediate release of Das and warned of a wider agitation if action was not taken. "If you do not take proper legal action immediately against those who have insulted my religion, then we will call for a larger movement in Bangladesh." Chowdhury further questioned the decision-making behind Das' arrest.He added that the minority communities would continue their protests if Das remained in custody. "The person who has been imprisoned must be released immediately. If he is not released, the Sanatan (Hindu) community, the Buddhist community, and the Christian community of Bangladesh will build a united movement on the streets in the coming days."The protests come amid continued concerns over the safety of minorities in Bangladesh. On June 23, India also raised the issue, urging Dhaka to act against extremist elements following reports of desecration of Hindu deities.Bangladesh's criminal investigation department (CID) had arrested Haridas on money laundering charges involving alleged suspicious transactions worth Tk 9.35 crore. The agency also claimed he had travelled illegally to India in 2010 and later converted to Islam in 2019, according to local media outlets.The arrest came months after the death of 25-year-old Hindu man Chanchal Bhowmik in Narsingdi, an incident that remains under investigation after CCTV footage raised suspicions of possible foul play.Addressing a media briefing in New Delhi, ministry of external affairs spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said, "We have seen reports from Bangladesh regarding acts of desecration involving Hindu deities and their images, which have sparked protests. We expect the Bangladeshi government to curb the extremists there and guarantee the safety of the minority community."
Article Summary: Minority leaders in Bangladesh staged a large protest outside the National press club in Dhaka. They condemned the arrest of a young Hindu man, Haridas Chandra Tarani Das. They alleged that the money laundering charges against him were fabricated to stop the construction of an 81-foot Lord Ram statue.

Article Title: Carer who fled to Spain after stealing £300k from pensioner to be extradited to UK
Article Text:
Pamela Gwinnett

A woman who fled to Spain after stealing nearly £300,000 from an elderly woman she cared for is set to be extradited to the UK to begin serving a six-year prison sentence, BBC reported.Pamela Gwinnett, 63, absconded to Tenerife while on bail ahead of her trial over the exploitation of Joan Green, 89, a retired accountant from Chorley, UK. She was convicted in her absence last October of theft and fraud by abuse of position after failing to return to court.Greater Manchester Police said Gwinnett has now been arrested in Spain and will be returned to Britain following a joint effort involving the National Crime Agency and the Spanish authorities.Investigators said she abused her position as Green's carer, gradually taking control of the pensioner's finances and isolating her from family members before draining almost £300,000 from her savings. Joan Green died in November 2022.Detective Constable Georgia Loughton, who led the investigation, said officers hoped Gwinnett's return would bring a measure of justice to Green's family as they continue to rebuild their lives.The court previously heard that Gwinnett left for Tenerife in April 2025 just hours after a judge refused her request to change her bail conditions so she could travel to the island, where she claimed she wanted to scatter her brother's ashes.During sentencing, Judge Michael Maher said Gwinnett had deliberately manipulated and exploited a vulnerable woman for financial gain. He described Green as having been treated like "a cash cow" and said the fraud had been carried out with "self-assured shamelessness".The judge said Gwinnett had spent years gaining Green's trust before isolating her and enriching herself at the pensioner's expense.As part of the scheme, Gwinnett made false allegations of theft and neglect against Green's stepdaughter and stepgrandchild before securing power of attorney over the victim's affairs.Stolen money was used to support an extravagant lifestyle, including the purchase of a £22,500 car and luxury beauty and Botox treatments.At the time of sentencing, Judge Maher said he hoped Gwinnett would eventually be returned to Britain, adding that allowing her to remain in Tenerife would be "an affront to justice and the rule of law".(This is a top Google Trends topic.)
Article Summary: Pamela Gwinnett, 63, absconded to Tenerife while on bail ahead of her trial over the exploitation of Joan Green, 89. She was convicted in her absence last October of theft and fraud by abuse of position after failing to return to court. Police said she has now been arrested in Spain and will be returned to Britain.

Article Title: 7 killed as Ukrainian drones hit Russian warehouses, oil depot near Moscow
Article Text:
The attacks marked another deep strike by Ukraine against infrastructure linked to Russia's war effort

Seven killed in Tambov warehouse strike

Oil depot fire, evacuations reported

Zelenskiy confirms logistics targets

Seven people were killed and 51 others wounded in a wave of Ukrainian drone attacks across Russia overnight, Russian officials said on Saturday. The strikes hit two warehouses belonging to Wildberries, Russia's largest online retailer, as well as an oil depot and a residential building. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy confirmed that Kyiv had targeted two major logistics facilities in the Moscow and Tambov regions, which he said were used to supply sanctioned components for drone production and navigation equipment.The attacks marked another deep strike by Ukraine against infrastructure linked to Russia's war effort. Moscow said its air defences intercepted 379 Ukrainian drones over 19 Russian regions, as well as Crimea, the Sea of Azov and the Black Sea.Seven night-shift workers were killed, and 25 people were wounded after Ukrainian drones struck a Wildberries warehouse in Kotovsk in the Tambov region, Governor Yevgeny Pervyshov said.The city is around 360 kilometres (220 miles) from the Ukrainian border and roughly 475 kilometres (295 miles) southeast of Moscow."Seven people working the night shift died on the spot," Pervyshov wrote on Telegram, adding that 28 drones were also shot down on approach."If they had achieved their goal, the number of civilian casualties could have been much higher," he said.A separate Ukrainian drone attack hit another Wildberries warehouse in Elektrostal, a city around 50 kilometres (30 miles) east of Moscow, Moscow region Governor Andrei Vorobyov said.Twenty-four people were wounded in the attack, taking the total number of people injured in the strikes to 51.Wildberries co-founder and CEO Tatyana Kim described it as a "terrible night" for Russia and the company, and offered condolences to the victims' families.In the Moscow region city of Noginsk, falling drone debris sparked a fire at an oil depot, Vorobyov said.Two people were wounded in the attack. A nearby maternity hospital and one residential building were evacuated as a precaution.The governor did not specify the extent of damage to the oil facility.Zelenskiy said Ukrainian long-range strikes had hit two "significant logistical facilities in the Moscow and Tambov regions"."These facilities were used by the aggressor to supply sanctioned components for the production of drones and navigation equipment," he wrote on Telegram.He also said an oil facility had been hit. Ukrainian special operations forces carried out strikes against targets in the Sea of Azov and occupied territory, he added.In the city of Vladimir, around 180 kilometres (110 miles) east of Moscow, a Ukrainian drone hit a residential building and sparked a brief fire, regional Governor Alexander Avdeyev said.No casualties were reported.The Russian defence ministry said its air defences had intercepted 379 Ukrainian drones overnight across 19 Russian regions, as well as Crimea, the Sea of Azov and the Black Sea.
Article Summary: Ukrainian drones strike two warehouses belonging to Wildberries, Russia's largest online retailer, as well as an oil depot and a residential building. Moscow said its air defences intercepted 379 Ukrainian drones over 19 Russian regions. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy confirmed that Kyiv had targeted two major logistics facilities in Moscow and Tambov regions.
